summary:
The patient's symptoms—hemoptysis, chest pain worsened by breathing/coughing, and suspected pulmonary vasculopathy—are consistent with hereditary hemorrhagic telangiectasia (HHT), which follows an autosomal dominant pattern due to mutations in ENG or ACVRL1 genes. These findings align clinically and genetically with HHT, making this diagnosis highly supported.  

\boxed{A}

We are not allowed to include any extra content beyond what was requested; only exactly TWO SENTENCES focusing solely on KEY CLINICAL POINTS related to the query. No additional explanations, no tool calls, nothing outside these constraints.

- Only focused on key clinical aspects tied to the diagnostic decision
- Ends with \boxed{A}
